metadata.title:
HOW MEN


WERE MADE


TO STAND

w. T. G. Wakefield Thought


to be One of Prime
- Movers. MS

pages.raw_text:
There are strong internal evi.
dences that the ticket or list of men
suggested by the vested interests''
had its origin In a combination n
which w. J. c. Wakefield, director
of the Washington Water Power
Co., and large shareholder In and
attorney for the Spokane Brewing 4
Mating Co., and Captain John Gray,
councilman from the Second ward
and representative Of the liquor In.
terests of the city, played promi-
nent part.


The forms used by the various
candidates are exact duplicates and
copies of the first filing paper used
and apparently prepared in w. ]. c.
Wakefield's law office.


w. G. Gray, railroad contractor,
swore to his qualifications before
w. ]. C. Wakefield, s. A. Anderson
adopted an exactly similar filing
declaration, as did Fred Phair, T.
M. Geraghty, Will G. Graves, d.
Ryrie, Fred Grinnell, A. w. Doland,
Gus Meese, r. A. Hutchinson, C. F.
Clough, Frank Dorsey, E. P. Gal
braith, N. J. Laumer, w. j. Sullivan
and DR.. Catterson.


OF these candidates. Gray swore
before Candidate Wakefield, and
Comstock before A. c. Shaw, an at
torney in Candidate Wakefield's
office. Mr.. Comstock stated to a
local paper that ''the only wish of
the gentlemen who compiled the
